what_it_does: A recall-generated mapping authored by 'recollection' that, for each demo (app-canvas, app-flow, app-sheet, scr-home, app-states, etc.), lists the authoring session id(s) and an intent snippet recovered by semantic recall over session histories (via :8007/pplx), each annotated with a cosine score. It is intended to feed DNA's interactive-app-face exploration and the lead's kimi-clone deep-reads by pointing each demo back to the conversation that authored it.
observations: Header explains the method: recall over session histories (:8007/pplx); per demo the authoring session(s) + an intent snippet; targets the lead's kimi-clone deep-reads; score=cosine.
Organized as one section per demo name, each listing up to three session UUIDs with a parenthetical cosine score and a truncated snippet of the recalled session text.

Recurring high-scoring session 7c2c1b74-... carries Tim's storage/portability intent: 'For storage, yes I think filesystem and yes I think making it portable and agnostic, addresses are very useful... It can move to supabase later if we want.'
Session 104a58a7-... recurs as build-worker / design-critic prompts for the 'Operable Interface' / 'Interactive Addressed Surface' loop (the autonomous self-modifying UI build).
Snippets are truncated mid-sentence (raw recall excerpts), mixing Tim's verbatim direction, agent build-worker prompts, design-critic prompts, task-notification stubs, and session-limit messages.
Cosine scores are modest (mostly ~0.40-0.54), reflecting loose semantic association between demo names and session content rather than exact provenance.

Covers many demo variants (app-canvas, app-flow / -fused / -full, app-sheet, app-scr-ledger / -focus / -compare, scr-home, scr-piece, scr-compose, scr-canvas, scr-dashboard, app-comp-cards / -controls, app-states / -2).
Some entries are clearly noise (session-limit notices, task-notification headers) surfaced by the recall rather than true authoring intent.
standouts: Tim's verbatim storage/portability principle recovered as the top intent for the canvas demos: filesystem-first, portable and address-agnostic, Supabase-later.
The method itself: tying frozen demos back to their authoring conversations by semantic recall + cosine score, as input for clone deep-reads.
conventions: Recall-grounded provenance with explicit cosine scores (the repo's measure-don't-infer discipline applied to demo->session attribution).
Honest about the tool used (:8007/pplx) and that snippets are recall excerpts.
Feeds a downstream consumer (DNA's explore + the lead's kimi-clone deep-reads) rather than concluding.
concerns: Low/moderate cosine scores and noisy snippets (session-limit lines, task-notification stubs) mean several demo->session attributions are weak and would need confirmation before being treated as true provenance.
Snippets are truncated, so the recalled intent is only partially legible from this file alone.
Same session id recurs across many demos, so the map points at broad authoring sessions rather than per-demo authorship.
notes: recollection's demo->authoring-session recall map — semantic (cosine-scored) provenance pointers from each demo to the conversations that likely authored it, as input for clone deep-reads; treat low scores as leads, not facts.
questions: Which of these demo->session attributions were confirmed by the downstream kimi-clone deep-reads versus discarded as low-confidence?
What do the truncated snippets say in full — do they actually express each demo's design intent or just co-occur semantically?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it is a recall-based demo-to-session-intent map for DNA's explore and the lead's deep-reads, and delivers exactly that (cosine-scored session pointers per demo).
apparent_themes: Recall-by-meaning provenance: tracing artifacts back to authoring conversations.
Filesystem-first, portable, address-agnostic storage (Tim's recovered principle).
Demos as outputs of an autonomous self-modifying UI build loop.
Honest, scored, tool-grounded attribution feeding downstream deep-reads.
intention_signals: recollection is trying to give each frozen demo a thread back to the conversation that authored it, so a downstream clone deep-read can recover the real design intent behind the demo rather than guessing — turning the demo set into a recall-navigable provenance map.
novelty: Using semantic session-recall with cosine scores as a provenance-mapping technique from artifacts (demos) to their authoring conversations, as explicit input for clone deep-reads.
contribution: Contributes the recall-grounded provenance index linking each interface demo to its likely authoring session(s) with cosine confidence, plus recovered Tim-verbatim intent snippets (notably the filesystem-first/portable/address-agnostic storage principle) — the demo-to-intent threading a fused system would keep to recover why each demo exists.
summary_for_embedding: recollection's recall-generated map linking each interface demo to its likely authoring session(s) via cosine-scored semantic recall, with recovered Tim-verbatim intent snippets, as input for the lead's kimi-clone deep-reads.
intention_for_embedding: To thread each frozen demo back to its authoring conversation so a downstream clone deep-read can recover the real design intent rather than guess.
